Title: Reset Filters - Search Results Preserve Search Term
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Search executed with term 'printer'
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and search for 'printer'
[WAIT] Wait for search results page
[ACT] Apply one or more filters on search results
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results
[ACT] Click 'Reset filters' button
[WAIT] Wait for filter reset
[ASSERT] Verify search term 'printer' remains in search bar
[ASSERT] Verify URL retains ?q=printer with no filter params
[ASSERT] Verify full search result set is restored
Assertions:
[STATE] Search input still contains 'printer' after filter reset
[NAV] URL contains ?q=printer; filter params removed
[MATH] Full search result count restored (not redirected to homepage or /catalog)
[DOM] No filter chips in chip bar
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on reset request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Search term cleared from input after reset; redirect away from search results; filter params remain in URL; console TypeError

Title: Search - GA4 search Event Fires on Search-Bar Submission
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
GA4 configured on storefront (production build — events fire; they do NOT fire in dev). The 'search' event is tied to the search-BOX interaction; it does NOT fire on a direct /search?q= URL load.
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] Read window.dataLayer and confirm it exists
[ACT] fill 'Search': 'hoodie'
[KEY] press [Enter]
[WAIT] Wait for the results page to render, then allow ~2-3s for the GA4 push to settle
[ASSERT] Read window.dataLayer and locate the gtag 'search' event
Assertions:
[STATE] window.dataLayer exists and is accessible
[STATE] dataLayer entries are gtag arguments-objects (entry['0']==='event', entry['1']===<event name>, entry['2']===params) — NOT {event:'...'} objects
[STATE] A 'search' event entry is present (entry['1']==='search') whose params (entry['2']) include search_term==='hoodie' (items_count and visible_items are also present; items_count may be 0 on the first push as it fires before results settle — do not fail on that)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] gtag('event','search',{search_term:'hoodie',...}) pushed to dataLayer
[CONSOLE] no GA4 or GTM errors
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: No 'search' gtag event after search-bar submission; event missing search_term; console TypeError

Title: Search - GA4 view_search_results Event on Results Page
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
GA4 configured on storefront (production build). view_search_results fires on both search-bar submit and a direct /search?q= URL load, after results load.
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/search?q=hoodie
[WAIT] Wait for the results page to fully load, then allow ~3s for the GA4 push to settle
[ASSERT] Read window.dataLayer and locate the gtag 'view_search_results' event
Assertions:
[STATE] dataLayer entries are gtag arguments-objects (entry['0']==='event', entry['1']===name, entry['2']===params)
[STATE] A 'view_search_results' event entry is present (entry['1']==='view_search_results') whose params (entry['2']) include search_term==='hoodie', a numeric results_count, and a numeric results_page (it carries results_count/results_page and visible_items — NOT items_count)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] gtag('event','view_search_results',{search_term:'hoodie',results_count,results_page,...}) pushed to dataLayer
[CONSOLE] no GA4 or GTM errors
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: No view_search_results gtag event after results load; event missing search_term; asserting items_count instead of results_count; console TypeError

Title: Buyer Journey - Autocomplete to PDP to Cart (Real-World Buy Flow)
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
User authenticated; cart at a known starting count (capture the live value before the add for defensive math); product-preview-in-dropdown is ENABLED (mini-run verified 2026-05-22; Did-you-mean OFF is fine — this case uses product previews, not text suggestions); B2B buyer-query pool seeded at test-data/search-queries/b2b-buyer-queries.csv with query_type=broad-noun rows that return >=1 result
Steps:
[NAV] Log in and navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[SETUP] read the cart badge count → {cart_count_initial}
[SETUP] read test-data/search-queries/b2b-buyer-queries.csv, filter rows where query_type=broad-noun, pick a random row → {picked_query}; record its query_id → {picked_query_id}
[LOG] write {cart_count_initial, picked_query, picked_query_id} to evidence
[ACT] Click the search input
[ACT] fill 'Search': {picked_query}
[WAIT] Wait up to 1500ms for the autocomplete dropdown 'Products' section to render
[ASSERT] Verify the dropdown 'Products' section is visible with >=1 product entry
[SETUP] browser_snapshot the dropdown 'Products' section; capture the first product entry's visible name → {picked_pdp_name}; capture its displayed price string → {picked_price_str}; parse numeric → {picked_price_num}; capture its href → {picked_pdp_url}
[LOG] append {picked_pdp_name, picked_price_str, picked_price_num, picked_pdp_url} to evidence
[ACT] Click the first product entry in the dropdown (autocomplete-click path — skip the results page entirely; mirrors how ~half of real buyers reach a PDP)
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to load
[ASSERT] Verify the PDP heading contains {picked_pdp_name}
[ASSERT] Verify the PDP displays a price (numeric, > 0); capture as {pdp_price_num}

Title: Cross-Facet AND Logic - Multiple Facet Groups Combine Correctly
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ORG_USER_DEFAULT]
Category page with multiple facet groups (e.g. Brand and Color) with overlapping product sets
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page with multiple facet groups on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for facet panel to load
[ACT] Select a value from the first facet group (e.g., a specific Brand)
[WAIT] Wait for filtered results; note the result count as Count_A
[ACT] Select a value from a second facet group (e.g., a specific Color)
[WAIT] Wait for combined filtered results; note the result count as Count_AB
[ASSERT] Verify Count_AB <= Count_A (AND logic narrows results)
[ASSERT] Verify all displayed products match BOTH the selected Brand AND the selected Color
[ACT] Remove the second facet filter (Color) by clicking its chip close button
[WAIT] Wait for results to update
[ASSERT] Verify result count returns to Count_A
[ASSERT] Verify only the Brand filter remains active
Assertions:
[MATH] Count_AB <= Count_A (cross-facet AND logic narrows the result set)
[DOM] All displayed products after both filters match Brand AND Color criteria
[STATE] Removing one cross-facet filter restores the single-facet result count
[MATH] No products appear that match only one of the two criteria
[DOM] Chip bar shows chips for both active facets
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql returns filtered results with correct AND combination
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Cross-facet returning OR logic (count increases or stays same); products matching only one criterion appearing; removing one facet not restoring single-facet count; console TypeError

Assertions:

[STATE] Reset filters restores fully in one action
[MATH] count_after_chip_removal == count_fg1_only (chip removal is precise, not full reset)
